evidence: The statement's hypothesis is that every arithmetic progression contains infinitely many integers which are the sum of distinct a_i.
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live, label exact; AP hypothesis (IsAPOfLength) is load-bearing

evidence: The conclusion 'every sufficiently large integer is the sum of distinct a_i' is the completeness property of the sequence.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live, label exact; conclusion (cofinite subsetSums) is exactly the (eventual) completeness property

evidence: The problem asks when 'every sufficiently large integer is the sum of distinct elements of A', i.e. when A is (eventually) complete.
verify_note: decl exists (research open); QID live; conclusion is eventual completeness of A — load-bearing

evidence: Cassels [Ca60] proved that under stronger hypotheses every sufficiently large integer is the sum of distinct elements of A.
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live; Cassels variant proves the same completeness conclusion

evidence: The problem asks whether the sum over any infinite A of 1/(2^n - 1) is irrational.
verify_note: decl exists (research open); QID live, label exact; Irrational is the entire question

evidence: The sum of d(n)/2^n equals the sum of 1/(2^n-1), the Erdős–Borwein constant, whose irrationality Erdős proved [Er48].
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live; sum d(n)/2^n IS the Erdos-Borwein constant — load-bearing

evidence: The statement is the irrationality of the sum of d(n)/2^n 'where d(n) is the number of divisors of n'.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; statement uses n.divisors.card explicitly

evidence: The series in question is the sum of d(n)/(a_1...a_n) 'where d(n) is the number of divisors of n'.
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live; d(n) appears explicitly in the series

evidence: The problem asks whether the sum of d(n)/(a_1...a_n) is irrational; proved affirmatively by Chojecki and GPT-5.4 Pro [Ch26].
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; Irrational is the conclusion

evidence: The series is the sum of mu(n)^2 * n / 2^n with mu the Möbius function (open scoped ArithmeticFunction.Moebius).
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live; mu via scoped ArithmeticFunction.Moebius, weight mu(n)^2 explicit

evidence: The problem asks whether the sum of mu(n)^2 n/2^n is irrational, proved by Chen and Ruzsa [ChRu99].
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; Irrational is the conclusion

evidence: The weight mu(n)^2 in the series is the indicator of the square-free integers.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; mu(n)^2 is precisely the squarefree indicator — the series is over squarefree n, load-bearing not scaffolding

evidence: The Behrend property asked for is that the set of multiples of {a+k : a in A} has natural density 1 ('almost all integers have a divisor of the form a+k').
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live incl. alias asymptotic density; IsBehrend is defined as HasDensity 1

evidence: The problem asks whether the sum of a_n/2^{a_n} is irrational whenever a_n/n tends to infinity.
verify_note: decl exists (research open); QID live; Irrational s is the conclusion

evidence: An irrationality sequence is defined by the sum of 1/b_n converging to an irrational number; part (i) asks whether 2^{2^n} is one.
verify_note: decl exists (research open); QID live; irrationality-sequence definition hinges on Irrational

evidence: Part (ii) asks whether every irrationality sequence (sums of 1/b_n irrational) must satisfy a_n^{1/n} tending to infinity.
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live; same irrationality-sequence concept

evidence: An irrationality sequence here requires the sum of 1/(a_n+b_n) to be irrational for every bounded perturbation b_n; part (i) is that 2^n is not one (Kovac-Tao).
verify_note: decl exists (research solved); QID live; Irrational of perturbed reciprocal sums is the core

evidence: Part (ii) asks whether n! is an irrationality sequence, i.e. whether the perturbed reciprocal sums are always irrational.
verify_note: decl exists (research open); QID live; same

evidence: The docstring asks 'Is n! an example of an irrationality sequence?', stated via Nat.factorial.
verify_note: decl exists (research open); QID live, label exact; statement is IsIrrationalitySequence Nat.factorial
